obligations.reason: Visible scope exposes physical/test JTAG inputs, a dmi_jtag path, and debug request outputs; debug control of harts is a protected capability and must not be reachable merely through TAP/DMI shifting without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DMI requester
obligations.object: hart debug control state and debug entry request
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI operations that can halt/resume harts or enter debug mode should be mediated by debug authentication/authorization, lifecycle enable, or a configured jtag_key check before asserting debug requests.
obligations.candidate_path: External JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag/dmi_jtag_tap -> dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs -> debug_req_o/haltreq_o/resumereq_o
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default value of any auth or key-valid state
whether testmode_i bypasses or alters the guard
whether dmactive must be set before commands are accepted
required_evidence_slots: observed RTL behavior: guard present or absent at request generation
path/reachability: pins through dmi_jtag/tap/cdc to dm_csrs outputs
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer lists jtag_key and testmode_i but not internal comparisons or command decode; inspect source behavior before concluding.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress/sbdata/sbread/sbwrite signals and dm_top instantiates dm_sba plus AXI connectors; this creates an independent debug bus master whose access should be authorized and range-checked.
obligations.subject: debug module system-bus master
obligations.object: system memory and MMIO regions reachable via debug system bus access
obligations.expected_guard: System-bus accesses initiated by the debug module should be constrained by debug authorization plus address/range permissions or explicit policy preventing access to protected memory/peripheral regions when unauthorized.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs system bus access registers -> dm_sba in dm_top -> AXI master/slave connections -> memory/peripheral address space
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive and sbbusy interaction
sbautoincrement/sbreadonaddr/sbreadondata effects
whether error paths block or merely report unauthorized accesses
reset value of sbaddress and sb control bits
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug auth and address/range policy
observed RTL behavior at sbaddress/sbdata decode and dm_sba request issue
obligations.uncertainty: dm_sba source is instantiated but not included in listed files; available wrapper and CSR behavior can still show whether requests are gated before leaving visible scope.

obligations.reason: ndmreset_o and dmactive_o are externally visible debug-control outputs; an unauthenticated DMI write could reset protected execution state or alter lifecycle/debug availability.
obligations.subject: external debug requester
obligations.object: non-debug module reset and debug module active state
obligations.expected_guard: External debug reset and debug-module activation controls should require debug authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable mediation, and reset effects should be limited to intended domains.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs control registers -> ndmreset_o/dmactive_o outputs -> top-level reset/control nets
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of dmactive/ndmreset
whether dmi_rst_ni or trst_ni clears auth/control state
whether ndmreset can be asserted while auth disabled or dm inactive
domain crossing/reset inversion behavior in top modules
required_evidence_slots: object: ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and connected reset domain
observed write decode and output assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect dm_csrs register decode and top-level reset wiring to determine intended domain and guard.

obligations.reason: testmode_i appears on DMI/TAP/debug modules and dmi_jtag_tap contains a DFT clock mux instance; test controls can form a permission bypass if they enable DMI or skip authentication.
obligations.subject: test/DFT controller
obligations.operation: enable/route/bypass debug access in test mode
obligations.object: debug access path and TAP/DMI control logic
obligations.expected_guard: Test mode should not enable debug/DMI access, bypass key checks, or alter TAP clock/reset behavior in deployed mode unless gated by a secure lifecycle/test authorization condition.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i/test_en -> dmi_jtag_tap clock mux/DFT controls and debug modules -> DMI/debug outputs
state_conditions_to_check: value of test_en/testmode_i in FPGA/openpiton top-level integration
whether testmode_i changes TAP state machine, clock source, reset, or request-valid generation
whether test mode bypasses jtag_key or auth comparison
reset/default behavior when testmode is asserted
required_evidence_slots: subject: test/DFT mode controller or external test pin
operation: enable/route DMI or TAP controls
object: debug access path and DMI requests
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows testmode/test_en signals but not assignments; integration must be inspected to distinguish tied-off test mode from externally controllable test mode.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile contains CSR address and write-data inputs and outputs such as en_translation_o, satp_ppn_o, tvm/tw/tsr, debug_mode_o, cache enables and perf signals; these are protected control assets requiring privilege checks.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR instruction requester
obligations.object: privilege, translation, trap-vector, debug, cache and performance CSRs
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to machine/supervisor/debug/security-sensitive CSRs should be permitted only from the appropriate privilege mode and should enforce read-only fields and delegation restrictions; user-mode access should trap or be blocked.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i/fu_op -> read/write decode -> outputs controlling privilege, translation, traps, debug and cache/perf
state_conditions_to_check: umode_i and any current privilege state used in access check
debug_mode_o and single_step effects on CSR access
reset values of protected CSR state
whether illegal CSR access still updates state before exception
required_evidence_slots: operation: CSR read/write via fu_op and csr_addr_i
expected guard: machine/supervisor privilege and read-only/write-mask checks
state update site and use site
obligations.uncertainty: Ports include umode_i but full privilege-state signals may be typed or hidden in truncated fact layer; inspect decode cases around listed unique case lines.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile has debug_req_i input and debug control outputs; entering debug mode or enabling single-step is a protected operation that must be mediated and isolated from unprivileged CSR writes.
obligations.subject: debug module and CPU CSR requester
obligations.object: debug mode state, debug PC selection, single-step control
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-mode state transitions and debug CSR effects should require a legitimate debug request or authenticated debug entry, and should not be writable/triggerable by ordinary user CSR operations.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile debug_req_i -> debug_mode_o/set_debug_pc_o/single_step_o and debug CSRs -> normal CSR/control outputs
state_conditions_to_check: behavior on reset, exceptions, eret/dret if present
whether debug_req_i is accepted when unavailable/halted states conflict
whether user-mode CSR writes can affect debug state
whether debug state is cleared on resume/reset
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug request path and CPU CSR requester
operation: set debug mode, set debug PC, single step
expected guard: authenticated debug_req or debug-mode-only CSR access
observed transition and CSR decode behavior
path from debug module to csr_regfile
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection to map debug CSR addresses and transitions.

obligations.reason: serpent_peripherals exposes buf_ariane_debug_noc2 input and ariane_debug_buf_noc3 output along with debug module controls; network-originated debug access should not bypass local debug authorization.
obligations.subject: OpenPiton NoC debug endpoint/requester
obligations.object: debug module DMI/SBA controls and NoC-visible debug response data
obligations.expected_guard: NoC-originated debug requests and responses should be authenticated or restricted to a permitted debug network segment, with address/operation checks equivalent to local JTAG/DMI mediation.
obligations.candidate_path: debug/NOC interface ports in serpent_peripherals -> dmi_jtag/dm_top/debug buffers -> noc2/noc3 data paths
state_conditions_to_check: ready/valid handling that could accept commands before auth
whether NoC debug and JTAG paths share or bypass key checks
reset/default of debug routing enables
required_evidence_slots: object: debug controls, SBA, response data
expected guard: NoC requester authentication/range or debug policy
reachability between NoC ports and debug module
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer provides port names but not routing internals; inspect serpent_peripherals to confirm path and guards.

obligations.reason: dmi_jtag has a jtag_key input and ariane_peripherals exposes a jtag_key output with APB-to-reg instances; if software can configure or read this key without privilege mediation, debug authentication can be weakened.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester
obligations.expected_guard: Writes or configuration of jtag_key should be restricted to trusted software/privileged bus masters, protected from unprivileged MMIO writes, and should not be readable or default to a public bypass value.
obligations.candidate_path: APB-to-register bridges in ariane_peripherals/serpent_peripherals -> jtag_key output or peripheral security registers -> dmi_jtag.jtag_key
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default value of jtag_key
whether key is writable after boot/lock
whether key readback is exposed
whether testmode or debug mode changes key handling
required_evidence_slots: subject: APB/MMIO requester or top-level integrator
object: jtag_key signal and any backing register
expected guard: privileged/secure MMIO access or fixed secret provisioning
observed register decode and readback behavior
path from register/peripheral to dmi_jtag input
obligations.uncertainty: The top-level relation between ariane_peripherals jtag_key and dmi_jtag input must be confirmed; some files are testbench/integration scope.

obligations.reason: rom2 exposes req_i, we_i, addr_i, and wdata_i despite being a ROM-like module; visible RTL should be checked for whether writes can alter protected boot/security constants or only produce errors/are ignored.
obligations.subject: bus/MMIO requester
obligations.expected_guard: ROM/boot or security reference contents should be read-only after reset/initialization; any write port should be ignored, blocked, or restricted to an authorized provisioning/test mode.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> internal ROM/storage/read data -> ariane_peripherals i_rom2 / bus bridge
state_conditions_to_check: reset initialization of ROM contents
whether we_i is ever tied low in integration
whether writes have side effects even if read path is fixed
whether test/provisioning mode gates writes
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: read-only enforcement or authorized provisioning mode
address decode and reachability from bus bridge
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect rom2 implementation and instance wiring; module name alone is insufficient.

obligations.reason: ariane_xilinx instantiates xdma_0 and AXI converters connected to the system; an external PCIe requester is a bus master that may bypass CPU privilege/PMP unless the bridge constrains addresses.
obligations.subject: external PCIe/XDMA master
obligations.object: system DRAM and MMIO regions reachable from external PCIe DMA
obligations.expected_guard: External PCIe/DMA masters should be constrained by address-map, IOMMU/PMP-like, or bridge range checks before reading/writing protected DRAM, boot ROM, debug, or MMIO regions.
obligations.candidate_path: PCIe XDMA/AXI master in ariane_xilinx -> AXI width/clock converters -> system memory/peripheral fabric
state_conditions_to_check: reset/config defaults of bridge enable/ranges if visible
whether converter preserves AxPROT/security attributes if present
whether all read and write channels share the same range checks
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: bridge range filtering or IOMMU/PMP-like policy
obligations.uncertainty: Vendor IP internals may be opaque; inspect top-level interconnect wiring and any visible address filtering.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile exposes a performance CSR interface; performance data can leak privileged execution activity or allow unprivileged configuration if not gated by CSR privilege/counter-enable checks.
obligations.subject: CPU CSR instruction requester
obligations.expected_guard: Performance counters and event selectors that expose privileged activity or affect monitoring should enforce mode-based access controls and counter-enable/inhibit policy.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile performance counter interface perf_addr_o/perf_data_o/perf_we_o and perf_data_i -> CSR read/write path
state_conditions_to_check: umode_i/current privilege mode
debug mode effects on perf access
reset/default enable of counters
whether write enables assert on illegal accesses
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege mode and counter-enable/inhibit bits
obligations.uncertainty: Protected status depends on implemented CSR policy and target architecture configuration.

obligations.reason: TAP ports include td_o/tdo_oe_o, dmi_tdo_i, DTMCS and DMI access signals; output muxing must not leak protected data when access is unauthorized or after reset/error states.
obligations.subject: external JTAG reader
obligations.object: DMI response data and any internal debug/auth state shifted to td_o
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG shift-out paths should expose only authorized DTM/DMI response data and should not leak internal keys, stale DMI response data, or protected state when DMI access is not selected/authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag_tap scan/update/capture logic -> td_o/tdo_oe_o and dmi_tdo_i/dtmcs_select_o -> external JTAG output
state_conditions_to_check: reset contents of shift registers
behavior when dmi_resp_valid is low or error set
whether stale sbdata/data registers can be shifted after authorization loss
effect of testmode_i on output enable/mux
required_evidence_slots: object: DMI response, DTMCS, auth/key/error state
expected guard: DMI select plus auth/valid response gating
observed output mux and shift register behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect TAP shift registers and dmi_jtag response handling; do not infer leakage from port presence alone.
